data.title: [create-dso-app]: plugin detection lookup path wrong -> installer aborts after clone
data.description:
## Incident Overview
User ran the one-command installer via `bash <(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/navapbc/digital-service-orchestra/HEAD/scripts/create-dso-app.sh)` with project name 'joe-test'. The script cloned the template, substituted PROJECT_NAME, started 'Installing npm dependencies...' and then exited with:

    Error: DSO plugin not found. Install via Claude Code marketplace or set CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT.

The trap on EXIT (line 411) removed the partial /Users/joeoakhart/joe-test directory.

## Root Cause (preliminary)
In detect_dso_plugin_root() priority-2 (marketplace path), the script checks:
    $HOME/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/digital-service-orchestra/.claude-plugin/plugin.json
But the marketplaces directory only contains marketplace.json at that path. The actual plugin.json lives at one of:
    ~/.claude/plugins/cache/digital-service-orchestra/<channel>/<version>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json  (cache)
    ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json  (marketplace internal)

Priority-3 (_PLUGIN_ROOT from BASH_SOURCE) also fails because bash <(curl ...) sets BASH_SOURCE[0]='/dev/fd/63' — not '/dev/stdin' — so the BASH_SOURCE branch executes and derives a nonsense _PLUGIN_ROOT of '/dev'.

## Observed Behavior
Installer exits 1 after npm install; project directory removed by EXIT trap; user has no artifacts.

## Expected Behavior
Installer locates the DSO plugin in the marketplace cache (or another valid marketplace layout location) and proceeds to DSO configuration.
